original_query: select ps_partkey, sum(ps_supplycost * ps_availqty) as value from partsupp, supplier, nation where ps_suppkey = s_suppkey and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'IRAN' group by ps_partkey having sum(ps_supplycost * ps_availqty) > ( select sum(ps_supplycost * ps_availqty) * 0.0001000000 from partsupp, supplier, nation where ps_suppkey = s_suppkey and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'IRAN' ) order by value desc;
rewritten_query:
WITH iran_partsupp AS (
    SELECT 
        ps_partkey, 
        ps_supplycost * ps_availqty AS product
    FROM partsupp
    JOIN supplier ON partsupp.ps_suppkey = supplier.s_suppkey
    JOIN nation ON supplier.s_nationkey = nation.n_nationkey
    WHERE nation.n_name = 'IRAN'
), total AS (
    SELECT SUM(product) * 0.0001 AS threshold
    FROM iran_partsupp
)
SELECT 
    ps_partkey, 
    SUM(product) AS value
FROM iran_partsupp
GROUP BY ps_partkey
HAVING SUM(product) > (SELECT threshold FROM total)
ORDER BY value DESC;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace implicit comma joins with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and clarity
Use a CTE to compute the joined data once instead of twice, reducing redundant processing
Pre-calculate the threshold value in a separate CTE to avoid repeating the same calculation
